channel.title: Help: Drafts Critique (new posts)
channel.description: Posts in the forum category "Help: Drafts Critique" - Want to run your draft past the veterans? Post here. Please read the forum guidelines, and post a wordcount for your drafts! Wait times vary depending on how crowded the forum is.

item.title: Criticism and Review: Criticism and Review
item.content:encoded:
Huzzah! I am finally ready to begin work on my first SCP!

I do not require green lights as I already have a +1 post on the main site (here)

Here it is:
http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/doggols-sandbox-ii

Under the tab 'Criticism and Review:**

My concerns:

  • Weirdness - Is it too hard to understand, can the mystery be solved?
  • Length - Is it too long or too short?
  • Story - Is the story behind it good enough?
  • Consistency - Is the anomaly consistent?

Fair warning, this is a Department of Misscomunications SCP, so It will be a bit confusing to readers. This is intentional.

spoilers, only click after reading
hide

Basically, a group of distinctly non-human individuals are messing with the Australian legal system. They've been using it to strike money out of people who break their laws copyright, and anyone who notices they're not human gets arrested for slander. These entities are incredibly tall, and leave a lot of mess wherever they go.

item.title: The Friendly Shadow: Re: The Friendly Shadow
item.content:encoded:
Got it… Sorry. This is my first time writing anything. So, I just need to continue working on execution? Perhaps remove -2 completely? Rewrite the interviews and security footage? I'm overall frustrated because I have no idea how to tackle this. I want to make this good, having read all of the guides regarding humanoids, but nothing is clicking in my brain. Do you have any further advice, or is it sick or swim?

Edit: Sorry again. Baby steps. I'll edit the things you mentioned in your crit. You've also inspired me on how to characterize them in the discovery portion. Thanks!

item.title: The Reality Dreamer: Re: The Reality Dreamer
item.content:encoded:
[+] Overall
[-] Overall

I don't hate this; and I think a lot of it's a fine start; but it feels…narratively lacking?

You said your main concern is the dialogue: beyond a few places where I've highlighted the flow of the conversation is weird, the actual like individual lines of dialogue and characters are all fine.

Indeed, generally the writing quality here is pretty alright.

Unfortunately, the big issue for me is there's not really a big hook: you theoretically have this interplay between the SCP being "dangerous" and the SCP being actually very friendly; where like, you have the containment logs indicating how big and dangerous this SCP is, contrasted with the relatively scared feeling in the first interview and the friendliness of the second. This doesn't land though: there's never a feeling of the SCP's effects actually having consequences; you mention things like 'the possibility that all individuals within the area have been affected' and 'the researcher was automatically woken after SCP-XXXX's range had breached thirty kilometers' but there's no grounding in this: we never see what effect the huge range or every individual in an area being affected actually has on the people, so the 'danger' of this is never established and the threat doesn't feel real.

Technically the hook could be the growth of the SCP: them like, becoming used to the situation and like, growing to harness their power better; but the problem is that it never feels like this is really shown. Like, the SCP definitely feels like they change a lot between the first and second interviews and it's not bad that this change happens, but it is bad that it's not shown to the reader.

I think the Containment Updates in the middle kinda makes this awkward for you. They're very detached from the consequences cos they're focussed on the containment; which makes conveying the scope of the danger hard with them; but they're also very focussed on what the Foundation is doing which makes conveying why the Foundation is doing stuff hard too; and so it doesn't really convey a sense of tension?

I feel like I have been quite harsh here: I like a lot of what this does conceptually, and I like the core of the story you're trying to tell here; I just don't think you've effectively told that story.

[-] Overall
[+] Line By Line
[-] Line By Line

Gonna note that I can miss stuff; flag things which are right as being wrong and indeed flag things which are deliberately wrong.
I'll likely use strikethrough to indicate stuff to remove and green text to indicate stuff to add.

SCP-XXXX must be kept at least thirty meters away from any humans or other members of the Hominidae family undergoing R.E.M. sleep while it is sufficiently contained by means of SCP-XXXX-01A.

This feels…overlong as a sentence? The part about 01A feels iffy like if it's sufficiently contained, why does it need to be 30m from other folk undergoing R.E.M. sleep? It seems like it's meant to be a separate thing?

SCP-XXXX's chamber is to be outfitted with a functioning nasogastric feeding tube and catheter.

I would probably refer to what the chamber is at least roughly somewhere before this (e.g. in the first sentence, using '…must be kept in a humanoid containment chamber at least…' say)

Any non-essential personnel of at least Level-2 security clearance are permitted to sleep within SCP-XXXX's range between the hours of 22:00 and 08:00 (subject to anomaly's discretion.)

Earlier you say it must be kept away from any humans in REM sleep: either non-essential personnel are allowed to sleep near it or that; you can't have both.

I'm a little hesitant at 'subject to anomaly's discretion' here too; it feels a little too much like the anomaly is being allowed to treat the foundation containment like a hotel?

SCP-XXXX is a white male of advanced age.

'advanced age' feels a little vague to me

functions inside a void-like sub-reality referred to as SCP-XXXX-01.

I'd probably use 'SCP-XXXX-1' here just to cut down a digit; and 'void-like' feels a little weirdly vague

within its range enters into the rapid eye movement stage of sleep.

you don't actually specify what this range is: the implication is 30m from the ConProcs but realistically there should be a margin of error on that (e.g., if the SCP's range is 10m, you want people 20m away just in case something happens and the range is bigger than expected or centred differently than expected)

SCP-XXXX-01A has been forcibly enlisted to counteract the properties of SCP-XXXX's body by improving the anomaly's overall mood and decreasing its influence.

you don't actually say what -01A is here and it feels…weird.

Document XXXX-01 is an official Foundation record, any unauthorized examination is punishable by termination.

I'd increase the size of this a touch; it's real tiny on my screen and almost a little hard to read.

I'm also not a fan of 'examination' here; it feels like 'access' might be better? Though termination feels harsh here too

What are you people doing here? Going in and out, get me out, please.

This feels, a little too all over the place? I think the second sentence needs just, a few more words to flesh out the ideas the SCP is trying to convey?

SCP-XXXX: I said let me speak.

The last thing the scp said was 'I want to talk, let's talk, I'll talk'; and it kinda feels a little too much like it means "I'll answer your questions then" in meaning for this to not feel a little jarring?

I require your full cooperation to make that come to pastpass.

Yes, well, several hundred people were trapped in here with you before we could get your physical form out of the city.

The 'Yes, well,' feels…out of place here? I can't quite put my finger on why but I think it's because the SCP hasn't like…asked a question or whatever.

Containment Update 20/11/15:

It feels weird to jump chronologically back to before the interview here

at the possibility of training Type Greens.

'Type Green' is GOC lingo and generally not what the Foundation calls 'em

[-] Line By Line

item.title: SCP draft- Ancient Nautiloids: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded:
Author, per the Draft Forum's new policy: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-10629348/new-draft-forum-policies authors who have not yet posted a successful mainsite article are now required to get their basic concept(s) greenlighted by two experienced reviewers in the Ideas Critique forum and/or IRC chatrooms before asking for draft feedback.

Please first go to the Ideas forum or the chat, give a quick summary (for the Ideas forum, please follow the instructions in the required reading thread) of the concept you want to write up (don't link the draft unless someone requests it), and reviewers can help you make sure that your core idea is something that will be successful with the community audience.

During the conceptualization stage, reviewers can also help by letting you know if something similar already exists on the site, and/or telling you if there are any cliches/pitfalls/other typical downvote reasons to be careful of with your particular idea. These issues are more effectively addressed earlier on in the writing process.

If you have already received the requisite greenlights, please note from whom and where you received them. If you would like to receive draft critique without going through the greenlighting process, please use the IRC chatrooms to request reviews.

item.title: Just trying to be cool again: Just trying to be cool again
item.content:encoded:
So after spending a considerable amount of time in IRC chat, I've come to notice a few things. A lot of people are more concerned with getting their ideas greenlit as opposed to crafting a good story. This is not the case for everyone, let me just preface that, but I do see it happen a fair amount of times.

This thought process is really upsetting to me. Why should anyone else's opinion be disregarded just because they can't give you a green light? Why would you think that your own thoughts are inadequate just because you can't give someone a greenlight? Doesn't make any sense to me, yet this seems to be the thought process for a lot of new authors on site. And many of them get discouraged and leave if their first idea doesn't work out.

Anyway, the preamble is over. I'm trying to convey all these emotions and such in the third tab of this sandbox. I'm really struggling with the subtlety of this one.

item.title: SCP draft- Ancient Nautiloids: SCP draft- Ancient Nautiloids
item.content:encoded:
STAFF NOTICE: According to the Draft Forum Guidelines, please do not post your entire draft in the forums. Make a sandbox page, and keep your draft there.

If the only copy of your draft is in this post, you can recover the text by clicking the 'Last edited on [dd/mm/yyyy], [time] by [username] Show more' section found in lower right corner of this post. Then click 'Show revision' to find the full draft from before. Copy that full draft and paste it into your sandbox page. Please note that 'Show revision' does not change the post back.

item.title: The Friendly Shadow: Re: The Friendly Shadow
item.content:encoded:
It sounds like I need to make it more drawn out

It's… not that this needs drawn out more per se so much as the areas that are drawn out need more careful consideration.

perhaps focus on a mission to retrieve Agent Wolf and explore -2.

I… wouldn't? The Wolf angle undercuts the main emotional impact, which is like, -1 feeling betrayed by the shadow; and overfocussing on what's effectively a subplot is a bad idea.

item.title: town of Alma: Re: town of Alma
item.content:encoded:
thank you very much for the crit!

+ show block
– hide block

I think you containment is direct and effective. I would word it only a bit different but I think they are good.

i'll implement your suggestions for the conprocs.

As far as the ritual goes I only ask myself some questions with the "anomalously modified strain of the herb". Is this an apart SCP? Is this just a modified plant that grew faster than normal? It might be a nitpick but I think this can easily be solved by adding a small amount of information or re-describing it.

the heather oil is a mostly unrelated anomaly that has to do with west, but the foundation doesnt know much about it beyond that she had something to do with its creation, so that's why the footnote refers the reader to a non-existent poi profile. i might specify that its thaumaturgically modified, because to me that better conveys the concept of the plant being "deliberately made and unknown"

I would label the entity which carries the portal (If my interpretation is correct) as SCP-XXXX-1. I would label the other pocket-dimension/Town of Alma (excuse my wording) something else.

this is a good idea, and ill definitely do that! i wasnt sure whether to give the entity a subdesignation when i was writing, but looking back on it now a lot of instances of the word "entity" definitely couldve been replaced with a subdesignation. i didnt give it a subdesignation before because i was worried about the article seeming like it had too many parts, but i figure just three things isnt too bad.

I assume a cot is a bed of linnen or something. English is not my native language. I would leave it as it is but consider "small bed, linnen bed, …" if people get confused by this. If they make no remarks on this, you should probably keep it.

apparently, cot is just an american term, which i didnt know! i'll change it to "folding camping bed" to be less confusing.

Again tone is not my specialty but I think this can be described differently. Suppression and offending don't seem completely out of place but changing a bit here can make it more clinical in my opinion.
I'd change "reportedly seen" to "seen" or "witnessed". I don't know about mentioning the Veil. Perhaps "were leaked to the public." is an option to consider.

i agree that i should reconsider some of the tone here! ill work on that.

Small nitpick: the tweet itself is ok but you have a typo (are instead of our).

this is intentional! the character got autocorrected. ill probably change the tweet anyway, though, because i just realized the time at the bottom is in the 12 hour clock. whoops.

I don't know if Class-A amnestics are enough but that is your choice. I personally go with Class-B most of the time. I know she kind of remembers since she almost literally spells it out in the addendum, but the Foundation probably won't take chances with taking amnestics like this. Perhaps you can mention she is somewhat resistant to amnestic since she comes from a branch in the Chaos Insurgency or just include a bit of thaumaturgy.

the amnestics were just to get rid of the memory of the foundation's investigation of her house and the questioning they put her through and all. west already lives in the "anomalous world" so more rigorous amnesticization wouldnt accomplish much. the implication with the "amnestic resistance" thing in the text log is that she has some sort of CI agent training that lets her know that she's been amnesticized at all, but the memories that were erased are still erased. she was just able to deduce what happened from the holes in her memory. ill probably specify this in a footnote since it's not exactly the most inuitive implication.

I'd expect texts to be shorter and have less punctuation marks etc.

the way "hubby" types is more a consequence of her personality than anything else. shes the kind of person whod use perfect grammar and punctuation in a text, no matter who its with

edit: ive made most of the edits i said i would in this post by now, as well as changing the object to Safe and some other wording changes

item.title: The Reality Dreamer: The Reality Dreamer
item.content:encoded:
http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/savanac02

Word count: 3.5k

Main Concerns:

  • The ending
  • Dialogue
  • Is the change in character warranted by the logs?

My first attempt at writing an SCP, time to overhaul it with some much need critique!

Both greenlights can be found here: http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/forum/t-13858907/the-reality-dreamer-seeking-greenlights

item.title: The Friendly Shadow: Re: The Friendly Shadow
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the review. I'll be sure to edit everything you mentioned and take this to heart. It sounds like I need to make it more drawn out and perhaps focus on a mission to retrieve Agent Wolf and explore -2. This could give a story of where XXXX came from and produce some worldbuilding.

I still have a long way to go.

item.title: town of Alma: Re: town of Alma
item.content:encoded:
Here we go.

Open
– hide block

I'd rewrite a few thing. Please bear in mind that tone isn't my specialty and these are just suggestions. You may copy them completely or just take elements where you see fit.

I think you containment is direct and effective. I would word it only a bit different but I think they are good.

…database, accessible to personnel with Level 3/XXXX clearance.

I'd stop the first sentence after "database" and then say something like: "Access is granted to…". I'd just split this in two sentences.

All other known copies have been destroyed, and newly discovered copies are to be destroyed as well.

I'd prefer something like: "New copies are destroyed upon discovery as all previously known copies."

As far as the ritual goes I only ask myself some questions with the "anomalously modified strain of the herb". Is this an apart SCP? Is this just a modified plant that grew faster than normal? It might be a nitpick but I think this can easily be solved by adding a small amount of information or re-describing it.

Then on to your main concern: the entity.

In the description you stated that SCP-XXXX-1 is an extradimentional space although I would label the entity which carries the portal (If my interpretation is correct) as SCP-XXXX-1. I would label the other pocket-dimension/Town of Alma (excuse my wording) something else.

I think your description of the entity and its counterpart/behind/… is good. I can visually imagine something close to what you describe. I just think the labeling could be better.

--

As far as the town itself I don't have any remarks. In the shed you have "anomalous heather oil."
I think here its description is not as confusing as the earlier but if you change anything about the plant, check if this is still consistent with the description.

I assume a cot is a bed of linnen or something. English is not my native language. I would leave it as it is but consider "small bed, linnen bed, …" if people get confused by this. If they make no remarks on this, you should probably keep it.

Information suppression was enacted before the offending tweets were reportedly seen by any individuals living outside of the Veil.

Again tone is not my specialty but I think this can be described differently. Suppression and offending don't seem completely out of place but changing a bit here can make it more clinical in my opinion.

…were reportedly seen by any individuals living outside of the Veil.

I'd change "reportedly seen" to "seen" or "witnessed". I don't know about mentioning the Veil. Perhaps "were leaked to the public." is an option to consider.

Small nitpick: the tweet itself is ok but you have a typo (are instead of our). If you can't fix this I can assume that it is an autocorrect.

I don't know if Class-A amnestics are enough but that is your choice. I personally go with Class-B most of the time. I know she kind of remembers since she almost literally spells it out in the addendum, but the Foundation probably won't take chances with taking amnestics like this. Perhaps you can mention she is somewhat resistant to amnestic since she comes from a branch in the Chaos Insurgency or just include a bit of thaumaturgy.

The addendum serves as an explanation and I think the format you used is fine. I don't 100% know how to crit a sms-conversation though. I'd expect texts to be shorter and have less
punctuation marks etc. but again they have that background of the insurgency so that it isn't too "loosely". I advise you to seek someone good with dialogue if you want to check tone on this. It is a good addition but doesn't completely hit for me/feels a bit off.

Overall I'd say whilst your draft is interesting and has some good content, you need to work on some loose ends and polish it here and there.

If you have to wait a lot for additional crit I advise you to go to the chat/IRC, I think you will find what you need there.

Good luck

item.title: The Friendly Shadow: Re: The Friendly Shadow
item.content:encoded:
[+] Overall
[-] Overall

I….don't really like this. I like what you're trying to do; but the actual execution feels all over the place: there's so much jumping around topics and themes that the core narrative feels muddier than it should.

Decisions like not introducing -2 until the midpoint, instead of mentioning it explicitly in both the Description and the Discovery contribute to this: it has a feeling of introducing new elements to force character growth to occur; whereas if all the pieces had been laid out at the start, you could just, have the characters react: the whole thing around -1 being the passenger for the shadow can come up naturally when the interviewer asks about the painting, instead of in this weird forced way.

The disappearance of Agent Wolf is a like, technically interesting plot thread that never feels like it goes anywhere: Wolf is brought up immediately as they disappear so we don't really get invested in bringing them back, but other than a token question to Thomas in the one interview, it's never really explored in any way and it really feels like it could be cut: the painting doesn't need to be made explicitly anomalous, it could be made interesting and asked about purely from the discovery log noting SCP-XXXX was watching it.

I think though the core issue from the storytelling is that all the interesting stuff is in the relation between Thomas and Clarence, but there's barely any times where they actually interact in a tangible way: the discovery and the short video log being the only real times; the first interview kinda does but in a disconnected way where the shadow is definitely interacting with Clarence but not in a way that we, the reader, can grok and so it doesn't really add to their relationship. And so the arc feels rushed: there's never really a chance for this to establish what their relationship is like for the reader as a baseline really, or for us to get a feel for their characters; and so when this relationship is shifted up by Thomas confessing to Clarence, we don't really have a way to predict what this will do. The other problem is that the entire 'revelation, fallout, attempt to reconcile' thing takes place in 500 words: it never really gets the time it needs to show how this affects either of the characters or for them to tangibly grow as a result of this; and so it feels…lacking.

I can definitely see this being rewritten to work better but…it feels to me like it'd need a really thorough editing pass where you really consider how the story flows a lot more and then really figure out how you can best convey the flow.

In it's current state, this would be a -1 from me, and I would guess most other readers would feel the same way.

[-] Overall
[+] Line By Line
[-] Line By Line

Gonna note that I can miss stuff; flag things which are right as being wrong and indeed flag things which are deliberately wrong.
I'll likely use strikethrough to indicate stuff to remove and green text to indicate stuff to add.

Following incident XXXX-A2, SCP-XXXX-1 has been provided with counseling and further education within Site-17.

I'd probably move the 'within Site-17' earlier into the ConProcs; perhaps right after noting it's kept in a humanoid containment chamber.

SCP-XXXX is the shadow of SCP-XXXX-1, a humanoid appearing as a 36-year-old male of Caucasian descent.

Caucasian descent means 'descended from someone from the Caucasus region'; I think you want e.g. 'white American male' here say instead.

I'm also a little iffy about introducing two SCP designations in the first 6 words of your SCP: it feels like perhaps introducing the shadow first and then later the human it's attached to would read better but?

SCP-XXXX-1 identifies as Clarence Carter.

'identifies' here implies 'this is a fake name'; I'd use 'is known as' say

SCP-XXXX has the illuminated outline of a mouth, eyes, and body similar to that of SCP-XXXX-1.

'similar to that' here implies -1 has an illuminated outline of these features too, I'd suggest a semi-colon before similar and perhaps 'similar in appearance' say?

SCP-XXXX is able to detach itself from SCP-XXXX-1 to interact with shadows of objects or people.

I feel like 'interact with other shadows' would be clearer here; and wouldn't have the weird implication that it can't interact with e.g. animals and plants

For more information, see Addenda.

I'd cut this; if you're not specifying a specific place that covers this, it's not necessary. Even if you are, it feels…odd? Like just trust a reader to assume you'll show this, y'know?

SCP-XXXX was discovered on December 3rd, 20██

Why does knowing the exact date this was discovered on pose a security risk? IMO: unredact this date, and later dates, so we have a concrete idea of the timeline that this is taking place over; as opposed to the current possibility of the discovery being up to 98 years before the rest.

Following SCP-XXXX-1's arrest at the Virginia State Museum, it was observed that SCP-XXXX-1 did not have a shadow.

I feel like it'd be a good idea to hint at why -1 was arrested here; y'know, use the crime to characterise what they're like.

Embedded Foundation agents reported the anomaly and removed it, amnesticised witnesses.

'the anomaly' is singular which implies they only removed one of SCP-XXXX and SCP-XXXX-1; and I'd be clear about which it is here.

The 'amnesticised witnesses' is too 'fragmentary'; it really needs a few more words e.g. 'before they amnesticised witnesses' say.

SCP-XXXX was contained by MTF Iota-10 ("Damn Feds"). The two objects were collectively designated as SCP-XXXX.

Not really a fan of the vague 'oh yeah a MTF just, contained the shadow' here; and also the two objects were not collectively designated as SCP-XXXX.

It should be noted that SCP-XXXX is referred as "Thomas" by SCP-XXXX-1.

<SCP-XXXX observes SCP-XXXX-2

-2 is defined ~750 words after this. This doesn't flow right as a result because we have no idea what's happening. Even just using 'observes a painting' with a 'later designated SCP-XXXX-2' footnote would be better.

you're also missing the closing > here

Possible that he’s holding back on details.

I'd add an 'It's' at the start here; this feels weirdly note like

He made a sudden change of opinion on the topic of whether to be separated from SCP-XXXX in further interviews when SCP-XXXX made an abrupt change of pace.

This is written here like it's summarising something that happened within the interview but it's not so it feels really weird? Like if it was summarising something, it'd be unnecessary cos we'd read it; but instead it appears to be info that Rose has just plucked out of the air.

LEVEL 3 CLEARANCE REQUIRED - INPUT CREDENTIALS

This feels altogether too short a note to be in a collapsible.

Agent Hart found Agent Wolf had disappeared while examining a painting on exhibit.

'on exhibit' feels weird to me; maybe 'in the exhibition' or 'on display' would be clearer?

Something about this construction is weird; it kinda implies Hart walked in, saw Wolf had disappeared and then just like, pointed at a random painting and said 'this did it'. It'd work better if there was more of a concrete link between this painting and the one referred to earlier but it feels like Hart should actually like…see this happening?

Efforts to retrieve Agent Wolf are in progress.

'underway' would be better than 'in progress' here.

After the retrieval of the anomalous painting

you've given it a designation by now, use the designation.

// <XXXX interacts

the space between // and < has broken your italics here.

//How about this. I'll answer anything you need to know, and you answer a few of my questions in return.

missing the closing // here

idea. And the thought of that alone terrifies me.
// <XXXX

space issue again; also missing an empty line between these

"Look, giving you answers will only lead to more questions. Questions you wish to have answers to. Some questions shouldn't be answered, Doctor."

you've got a big chunk of this interview repeating here a few times; it's very weird and I think accidental?

[-] Line By Line

item.title: SCP-XXXX - 4D Pocket: Forum Criticism Team - Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Bexided, instead of replying to your own comments, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

item.title: Mandala-Manifested Meadow of Majestic, Melancholic Madness: Re: Mandala-Manifested Meadow of Majestic, Melancholic Madness
item.content:encoded:
+ Crit lives within
– hide block

Coherence:

Assuming you meant thematic coherence (due to the Understandability section), this article is a bit all over the place. The nuns, the worms, the mass hysteria, etc., none of it feels like it's properly fit together. Moreover, the Mandala's usage feels largely superficial, especially considering that it felt like a Hindu concept was being forced into a largely Christian framework.

Consistency

Everything from the tulpa onward feels as if it's missing context. The stakes necessary for it to work simply aren't there for much of the piece, and it leads to what feels like a slog through events that don't carry any emotional weight.

Dialogue

Dialogue was alright. Only thing of note was that I found it odd:

Operator: Indeed. The phone says that this fluid's effects include but are not limited to:
1.) Loss of inhibitions.
2.) Steady increase in psychosis-type symptoms.
3.) Dizziness.

Having a character suddenly speak in list form feels off.

Clinical tone

It was fine.

Pacing

The pacing is incredibly off. By the IV portion, the piece read like it was near the end, despite being only 1/3rd the way through. It made the chase scene a major slog. Then the dream ends, and it cuts to the second offset, which feels almost entirely disconnected from the events I read in the log. It's for this reason among others that I recommend removing the second offset.

Additionally, how the "FOOTAGE LOST"s break up sections is very disorientating. It leaves a lot of the earlier sections feeling cut off from one another, without a sense of continuity.

Plot

It didn't seem very good.

As noted above, the piece fails to establish the emotional stakes. I don't know who Hero is; I don't know who the tulpa is. Because I as the reader have no real understanding of these characters, I'm not able to care about everything happening to them. The end result is that the plot feels like a slog through a list of things occurring to characters I neither know or care for.

Additionally, none of the plot feels like it's thematically connected to the anomaly at hand. This is a piece about someone attempting to rescue their tulpa from the dreamscape for a good chunk; however, the overarching "eldritch sponge" aspect barely comes up, and it's only really explored in what amounts to a (figurative) footnote in the final offset. This is especially bad as I heavily recommend you remove it for pacing, among other things.

Understandability

I could understand what was happening perfectly fine, up until the final offset. I don't have any advice on how to fix said offset's understandability, as again, I heavily recommend you remove it.

Is the second list-page even necessary, or can I just remove it and shove the plot reveal into the main page via a collapsible?

100% remove it. It feels overly expository, and the note at the end is unnecessary.

Overall

This piece needs extensive restructuring if it wants to work. The thematics don't feel consistent, the piece feels like it's ending a third of the way through, and the characters don't make enough of an impression to justify the plot of the log.

item.title: SCP Draft - A Sleep Paralysis Demon: Re: SCP Draft - A Sleep Paralysis Demon
item.content:encoded:
Overall, the idea feels a bit uninspired. Simply making a sleep paralysis demon an SCP can definitely work, but it could use a bit more originality or a spin of some kind.

+ Some Critique
- Begone

If you are looking to add to the atmosphere or imagery in your article, one really easy way to do this is to have an interview with an individual who experienced the SCP. After the description, include an interview with a civilian or a member of Foundation staff that was affected. (Perhaps this individual had a unique experience with the SCP, thus causing an interview to become necessary) This way you can more easily put the reader in a first person perspective and allow them to experience the horror and imagery you are going for. This doesn't have to be particularly lengthy either.

UPDATE: Following recent discoveries of biological evidence at SCP-XXXX manifestation points, SCP-XXXX is to be re-classified as Explained (See Case Report - XXXX for further details).

Further investigations are to be left solely to the Chicago Police Department.

I'm not sure what you're going for here, although it could just be me. What kind of biological evidence was found at the manifestation points, and why does it warrant a reclassification and handing the investigation over to the police? In my mind, this implies that the SCP is actually a human or group that's doing these bizarre things for some unknown reason. If this is simply meant to be some kind of cliffhanger/ mystery that's left up to the reader's imagination, then it could be made a bit more clear through wording. If this is indeed suppose to imply that there's a human group or individual doing these things, then it would probably be directly stated in the addenda and description.

item.title: SCP Draft - A Sleep Paralysis Demon: SCP Draft - A Sleep Paralysis Demon
item.content:encoded:
http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/penton (A Sleep Paralysis Demon)

I'm mostly looking for advice on how to improve the overall atmosphere and disturbing imagery without making the piece too long. As well as what additional details I can add for the fridge horror and just general creepiness.

Hopefully designed to be short, simple, but leave a bad taste in your mouth.

Feedback is welcome as always!

item.title: The Black Hourglass: Re: The Black Hourglass
item.content:encoded:
I apologize for not making it clear. The containment breach was not caused by Licht. I'll make sure to revise this and make it clearer.

This actually didn't come when choosing the name Licht, and I didn't intend for it to be a reference to the undead immortal sorcerer/s. The cultural origin of the name is unrelated as well, so it seems to be a coincidence.

item.title: SCP-XXXX - 4D Pocket: Forum Criticism Team - Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Bexided,

As per the Draft Forum Policies, new authors are to get their concepts reviewed and greenlighted by two experienced reviewers on the Idea Critique Forums before posting on the Draft Critique Forums.

During the conceptualization stage, reviewers can give assistance in developing an idea before a draft is written. They can also assist in letting you know if something similar exists on the site already, or inform you of various clichés/pitfalls to be cautious of. These elements are more effectively addressed earlier in the writing process.

If you would like to request a draft review without greenlights, you can use the IRC Chatrooms to solicit critique.

If you have already received the requisite greenlights, please note the thread in which you received them, or the users whom provided them.

item.title: SCP-XXXX - 4D Pocket: Re: SCP-XXXX - 4D Pocket
item.content:encoded:
Try to ignore the code up there, I wrote it thinking it would show up properly. Alsosince I didnt know how to actually write it I copied and pasted it from 527.
.

item.title: SCP-XXXX - 4D Pocket: SCP-XXXX - 4D Pocket
item.content:encoded: no full drafts in the forum
